verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a trans-Golgi network (TGN) resident transmembrane protein involved in intracellular cargo sorting. It is stably retained in the TGN via retrieval mechanisms and lacks any documented evidence of surface trafficking under baseline, stress, or activation conditions. No cell-surface-directed therapeutics target it. Ruled out: cell-state-induced surface display (no stress/activation evidence), tissue-restricted (ubiquitous TGN housekeeping protein), lysosomal exocytosis (TGN, not lysosomal), dual localization (no PM pool documented), stable surface attachment (not secreted). Classic endomembrane resident.
